findings.title: CI self-scan exceptions applied to local scan
evidence: CI env var not set — this appears to be a local scan
requiredActions: CI self-scan exceptions (.github/security-exceptions-ci.json) are being applied to a local scan. This suppresses 7 controls. Set SECURITY_GATE_EXCEPTIONS to point to your project's exceptions file.

finding.title: No SBOM (Software Bill of Materials) found in repository
evidence: Searched for CycloneDX (.cdx.json, bom.json, bom.xml) and SPDX (.spdx, .spdx.json) files.
requiredActions: Generate an SBOM in CycloneDX or SPDX format and commit it to the repository.
For Node.js: `npx @cyclonedx/cyclonedx-npm --output-file sbom.json`.
For Python: `cyclonedx-bom -o sbom.json`.
Automate SBOM generation in CI so it stays current with every dependency change.
Ensure the SBOM is included in any artifact upload to your registry (SLSA Level 2+).

finding.title: Payment/auth files changed but no pentest report or sign-off found
evidence: Changed files triggering this check: src/gate/checks/auth-deep.ts, src/mcp/auth.ts
Searched .mcp/ and security/ for pentest-report* — no match found.
requiredActions: Obtain a pentest sign-off for payment and authentication flows before shipping.
Place the report as pentest-report-<date>.md (or .pdf) in .mcp/ or security/.
The report must cover OWASP Top-10 auth/session flaws, insecure direct object references, and PCI DSS requirements 6.3-6.5.
If a full pentest is not yet complete, document interim risk acceptance with CISO sign-off.

finding.title: Compliance-related files changed but no compliance gap analysis found
evidence: Changed files triggering this check: src/gate/policy.ts
Searched .mcp/, security/, and docs/ for compliance-gap* or compliance-findings* — no match found.
requiredActions: Produce a compliance gap analysis document before merging compliance/policy changes.
Place it as compliance-gap-<date>.md in .mcp/, security/, or docs/.
The gap analysis must map each changed control to its framework requirement (HIPAA §164, PCI DSS 4.0, GDPR Art. 32, etc.).
Document any residual risk and obtain sign-off from the compliance owner.

finding.title: Compiled dist/ directory excluded from secret scan
evidence: dist/ directory exists but is excluded from secret scanning
requiredActions: Manually inspect dist/ for secrets injected by build tools such as webpack DefinePlugin or Vite define.
Ensure secrets are not inlined into compiled bundles via build-time substitution.
Consider adding a targeted scan of dist/ for high-confidence patterns (API key prefixes, PEM headers) in CI.

finding.title: Hardcoded secret detected: process.env fallback with hardcoded secret value
requiredActions: Remove the process.env fallback with hardcoded secret value from source code immediately.
Rotate the exposed credential — treat it as compromised.
Store the secret in your cloud secret manager (AWS Secrets Manager, GCP Secret Manager, Azure Key Vault, HashiCorp Vault, Doppler, or 1Password Secrets Automation).
Add a pre-commit hook or CI check with gitleaks to prevent future secret commits.

finding.title: Secret detected in git history (3 findings)
requiredActions: Secrets in git history remain exposed even after removal from the working tree.
Rotate all exposed credentials immediately.
Use git-filter-repo or BFG Repo-Cleaner to purge the secrets from history, then force-push and notify all collaborators to re-clone.
Enable branch protection and secret scanning alerts on the remote host.

finding.title: Dependency "execa" has a low OpenSSF Scorecard score (4.0/10)
requiredActions: Review the OpenSSF Scorecard for execa at https://scorecard.dev.
Consider replacing low-scored dependencies or accepting documented risk.

finding.title: Dependency "fast-glob" has a low OpenSSF Scorecard score (3.2/10)
requiredActions: Review the OpenSSF Scorecard for fast-glob at https://scorecard.dev.
Consider replacing low-scored dependencies or accepting documented risk.

finding.title: 1 scoped package(s) lack a private registry entry in .npmrc — dependency confusion risk
evidence: @modelcontextprotocol/sdk (scope @modelcontextprotocol has no private registry entry in .npmrc)
requiredActions: Scoped packages without a private registry mapping in .npmrc will resolve from the public npm registry, enabling dependency confusion attacks.
ATT&CK T1195.002 — an attacker can publish a higher-versioned package to the public registry under your private scope name.
Fix: add to .npmrc: @yourscope:registry=https://your-private-registry.example.com

finding.title: Required security scanners are not installed or not runnable
requiredActions: Install the missing scanners or adjust the approved scanner config intentionally.
Do not rely on heuristic checks alone when fail-closed scanner enforcement is enabled.

finding.title: Security headers not found (CSP/HSTS/etc.)
requiredActions: Add strict security headers: CSP (no inline JS), HSTS, X-Frame-Options, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy.
Enforce secure cookies: HttpOnly, Secure, SameSite, short-lived tokens.

evidence: defaults/checklists/web.json:13:    { "id": "web_xss_no_dsi", "description": "dangerouslySetInnerHTML absent or sanitized with proven HTML sanitizer", "critical": true },

.mcp/baselines/0ef307bb4a01d514ff6ab3e50e73bbf5d34a564d.json:1231:            "defaults/checklists/web.json:13:    { \"id\": \"web_xss_no_dsi\", \"description\": \"dangerouslySetInnerHTML absent or sanitized with proven HTML sanitizer\", \"critical\": true },",

.mcp/baselines/0ef307bb4a01d514ff6ab3e50e73bbf5d34a564d.json:1232:            "security/checklists/release-web.md:39:- [ ] dangerouslySetInnerHTML absent OR sanitized with proven HTML sanitizer (DOMPurify)",

.mcp/baselines/0ef307bb4a01d514ff6ab3e50e73bbf5d34a564d.json:1233:            "security/checklists/release-web.md:164:- [ ] dangerouslySetInnerHTML, open redirect, and prototype pollution patterns from prior runs confirmed fixed",
requiredActions: Remove dangerouslySetInnerHTML where possible.
If unavoidable: sanitize with a proven HTML sanitizer and add unit tests with XSS payloads.

finding.title: Server-side fetch patterns detected. SSRF protections must be enforced.
evidence: .github/security-exceptions-ci.json:110:      "justification": "False positives from patterns matched in non-application files: DANGEROUSLY_SET_INNER_HTML matched in .mcp/baselines/ JSON data (a stored scan result, not code); SSRF_GUARD_REQUIRED matched 'undici-types' in package-
skills/agentic-loop-exploiter/SKILL.md:231:   Search for `fetch(`, `requests.get(`, `axios.get(`, `nodeFetch`. Finding: any network call without

skills/bot-detection-specialist/SKILL.md:244:- **JA3/JA4 TLS Fingerprint Mismatch for Headless Client Detection (Research: Salesforce JA3 2017, BLAKE2 JA4 2023):** Automated HTTP clients (`curl`, `python-requests`, Go `net/http`, Node `undici`) produce TLS ClientHello JA3 hashes disti
skills/capec-code-mapper/SKILL.md:74:| Dynamic URL in fetch() | CAPEC-94 | Adversary in the Middle | CWE-918 | Check for URL allowlist |
requiredActions: Implement SSRF guard for any server-side HTTP client: block localhost, private IP ranges, and cloud metadata endpoints.
Require URL allowlists for outbound calls. Add tests for 127.0.0.1, 10/8, 172.16/12, 192.168/16, 169.254.169.254, metadata.google.internal.

requiredActions: After fetching a resource by URL param, verify the authenticated user owns or is authorised to access it.
Never rely on obscurity of IDs — enforce ownership checks server-side.
Use opaque, non-guessable IDs (UUIDs) and still enforce access control.

finding.title: GraphQL introspection enabled without NODE_ENV guard
requiredActions: Disable GraphQL introspection in production: `introspection: process.env.NODE_ENV !== 'production'`.
Introspection exposes the full API schema to attackers and aids targeted exploitation.
Consider also disabling GraphQL Playground / Sandbox in production.

finding.title: Potential PII or sensitive fields logged in server-side code
evidence: src/mcp/server.ts:2058:    fix: "const { password, token, ...safeFields } = req.body;\nconsole.log({ requestId, safeFields })",
requiredActions: Never log PII (email, password, token, SSN, card number, CVV, date-of-birth) at any log level.
Strip sensitive fields before logging: log only IDs, timestamps, and non-sensitive metadata.
Replace logged secrets with [REDACTED] and add a lint rule (eslint-plugin-no-secrets) to enforce this.

finding.title: Session configuration missing secure: true, httpOnly: true, or sameSite
requiredActions: Set secure: true so cookies are only sent over HTTPS.
Set httpOnly: true to prevent JavaScript access to session cookies (mitigates XSS theft).
Set sameSite: 'strict' or 'lax' to prevent CSRF attacks.
Also set a short maxAge (e.g. 15–60 minutes for sensitive sessions) and regenerate the session ID after login.

finding.title: Overprivileged IAM role or wildcard permission detected
evidence: README.md:559: - Wildcard IAM `"Action": "*"` -> least-privilege policy with specific actions
.mcp/baselines/0ef307bb4a01d514ff6ab3e50e73bbf5d34a564d.json:1437:             "Replace Owner/Contributor/Editor bindings with purpose-scoped custom roles.",
requiredActions: Apply least-privilege to every IAM role — enumerate only the specific actions and resources required.
Replace wildcard actions ('*') with explicit action lists.
Replace Owner/Contributor/Editor bindings with purpose-scoped custom roles.
Run IAM Access Analyzer (AWS) or Policy Analyzer (GCP) to detect unused permissions.

requiredActions: Restrict ingress to known CIDR ranges or private VPC subnets only.
Place public load balancers in a DMZ; never expose internal services directly.
Enable S3 Block Public Access at the account level.
Use Zero Trust network access (BeyondCorp / Zscaler / Cloudflare Access) instead of IP allowlisting.

finding.title: Audit logging or deletion protection explicitly disabled in IaC config
evidence: .mcp/baselines/0ef307bb4a01d514ff6ab3e50e73bbf5d34a564d.json:82:           ".mcp/baselines/0ef307bb4a01d514ff6ab3e50e73bbf5d34a564d.json:86:           \".mcp/baselines/latest.json:76:           \\\"skills/aws-penetration-tester/SKILL.md:411:    is absent or `enable_logging = false`, the account has no fo

.mcp/baselines/latest.json:82:           ".mcp/baselines/0ef307bb4a01d514ff6ab3e50e73bbf5d34a564d.json:86:           \".mcp/baselines/latest.json:76:           \\\"skills/aws-penetration-tester/SKILL.md:411:    is absent or `enable_logging = false`, the account has no fo
requiredActions: Enable audit logging on all cloud resources and ship logs to a centralised, tamper-evident store.
Enable deletion protection on databases and stateful resources.
Retain audit logs for at least 1 year (SOC 2 / PCI DSS requirement).

finding.title: GraphQL introspection is explicitly enabled without a NODE_ENV guard
requiredActions: Disable introspection unconditionally in production.
Use `introspection: process.env.NODE_ENV !== 'production'` at minimum.

finding.title: No GraphQL query batching limits detected
requiredActions: Configure batching limits to prevent batch-based DoS attacks.
Limit the number of operations per batch request.

finding.title: No GraphQL query complexity limiter detected
requiredActions: Add graphql-query-complexity or graphql-cost-analysis to limit query cost.
Set a maximum complexity budget to prevent amplified alias abuse.

requiredActions: Always use sslmode=require or sslmode=verify-full for PostgreSQL.
Never disable TLS for database connections — transmits credentials and data in plaintext.

finding.title: Database connection pool initialized without explicit limits
requiredActions: Set connection pool limits (max, min) to prevent resource exhaustion.
Unbounded pools can crash the database under load or be exploited for DoS.

requiredActions: Use parameterized queries or ORM query builders — never concatenate user input into SQL.
CWE-89: SQL injection can lead to full database compromise.

finding.title: Weak hash algorithm (MD5/SHA-1) detected
requiredActions: Use SHA-256 minimum (SHA-3 recommended for new code).
MD5/SHA-1 are broken for security purposes (NIST SP 800-131A Rev 2).

finding.title: SHA-256/SHA-512 are fast hash functions unsuitable for password storage. Use bcrypt, argon2, or scrypt.
evidence: .mcp/baselines/0ef307bb4a01d514ff6ab3e50e73bbf5d34a564d.json:1680:            ".mcp/baselines/0ef307bb4a01d514ff6ab3e50e73bbf5d34a564d.json:2292:            \"Replace with crypto.createHash('sha256') for integrity, scrypt/argon2 for passwords, and AES-256-GCM for symmetric encryption.\"",

.mcp/baselines/0ef307bb4a01d514ff6ab3e50e73bbf5d34a564d.json:2335:            "Replace with crypto.createHash('sha256') for integrity, scrypt/argon2 for passwords, and AES-256-GCM for symmetric encryption."

.mcp/baselines/6abc94bb2077edde5907976bdb9f280a931c5027.json:958:            "Replace with crypto.createHash('sha256') for integrity, scrypt/argon2 for passwords, and AES-256-GCM for symmetric encryption."

.mcp/baselines/latest.json:1680:            ".mcp/baselines/0ef307bb4a01d514ff6ab3e50e73bbf5d34a564d.json:2292:            \"Replace with crypto.createHash('sha256') for integrity, scrypt/argon2 for passwords, and AES-256-GCM for symmetric encryption.\"",
.mcp/baselines/latest.json:2335:            "Replace with crypto.createHash('sha256') for integrity, scrypt/argon2 for passwords, and AES-256-GCM for symmetric encryption."
skills/algorithm-implementation-reviewer/SKILL.md:48:   - `createHash('sha256').update(password)` — NOT a password hash → immediate CRITICAL
requiredActions: Replace SHA-based password hashing with bcrypt (cost ≥ 12), argon2id, or scrypt.
Fast hash functions allow billions of guesses per second with GPU hardware.

finding.title: Non-cryptographic random used in security-sensitive context
evidence: README.md:431:| **Cryptography** | MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, ECB mode, `Math.random()` for tokens, short JWT secrets |
.mcp/baselines/0ef307bb4a01d514ff6ab3e50e73bbf5d34a564d.json:1708:            "README.md:431:| **Cryptography** | MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, ECB mode, `Math.random()` for tokens, short JWT secrets |",

.mcp/baselines/0ef307bb4a01d514ff6ab3e50e73bbf5d34a564d.json:1710:            ".mcp/baselines/0ef307bb4a01d514ff6ab3e50e73bbf5d34a564d.json:1695:            \"README.md:431:| **Cryptography** | MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, ECB mode, `Math.random()` for tokens, short JWT secrets |\",",

.mcp/baselines/0ef307bb4a01d514ff6ab3e50e73bbf5d34a564d.json:1716:            ".mcp/baselines/0ef307bb4a01d514ff6ab3e50e73bbf5d34a564d.json:1712:            \"Math.random() is not cryptographically secure and must never be used for tokens, keys, or nonces.\"",

.mcp/baselines/0ef307bb4a01d514ff6ab3e50e73bbf5d34a564d.json:1717:            ".mcp/baselines/6abc94bb2077edde5907976bdb9f280a931c5027.json:730:            \"README.md:431:| **Cryptography** | MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, ECB mode, `Math.random()` for tokens, short JWT secrets |\","
.mcp/baselines/0ef307bb4a01d514ff6ab3e50e73bbf5d34a564d.json:1726:            "Math.random() is not cryptographically secure and must never be used for tokens, keys, or nonces."
requiredActions: Use crypto.randomBytes() (Node.js) for security-sensitive randomness.
Math.random() is not cryptographically secure and must never be used for tokens, keys, or nonces.

finding.title: Non-cryptographic random used to generate identifiers or paths — predictable IDs enable enumeration attacks
requiredActions: Use crypto.randomBytes() or crypto.randomUUID() for generating IDs, paths, and codes.
Predictable identifiers enable IDOR and enumeration attacks.

finding.title: HS256/HS384/HS512 JWT algorithm detected — symmetric key shared with all verifiers
requiredActions: Use RS256 or ES256 for stateless JWTs.
HS256 requires sharing the secret with every verifier — use asymmetric algorithms instead.

finding.title: ECDSA P-256 detected — quantum-vulnerable in the long term
evidence: .mcp/baselines/0ef307bb4a01d514ff6ab3e50e73bbf5d34a564d.json:1809:            ".mcp/baselines/0ef307bb4a01d514ff6ab3e50e73bbf5d34a564d.json:1802:            \"P-256 (secp256r1) is secure today but vulnerable to Shor's algorithm on a sufficiently large quantum computer.\",",

.mcp/baselines/0ef307bb4a01d514ff6ab3e50e73bbf5d34a564d.json:1810:            ".mcp/baselines/6abc94bb2077edde5907976bdb9f280a931c5027.json:787:            \".mcp/baselines/6abc94bb2077edde5907976bdb9f280a931c5027.json:779:            \\\"P-256 (secp256r1) is secure today but vulnerable to Shor's algorith

.mcp/baselines/0ef307bb4a01d514ff6ab3e50e73bbf5d34a564d.json:1811:            ".mcp/baselines/6abc94bb2077edde5907976bdb9f280a931c5027.json:788:            \".mcp/baselines/latest.json:779:            \\\"P-256 (secp256r1) is secure today but vulnerable to Shor's algorithm on a sufficiently large quantum
.mcp/baselines/0ef307bb4a01d514ff6ab3e50e73bbf5d34a564d.json:1818:            "P-256 (secp256r1) is secure today but vulnerable to Shor's algorithm on a sufficiently large quantum computer.",

.mcp/baselines/6abc94bb2077edde5907976bdb9f280a931c5027.json:787:            ".mcp/baselines/6abc94bb2077edde5907976bdb9f280a931c5027.json:779:            \"P-256 (secp256r1) is secure today but vulnerable to Shor's algorithm on a sufficiently large quantum computer.\",",

.mcp/baselines/6abc94bb2077edde5907976bdb9f280a931c5027.json:788:            ".mcp/baselines/latest.json:779:            \"P-256 (secp256r1) is secure today but vulnerable to Shor's algorithm on a sufficiently large quantum computer.\",",
.mcp/baselines/6abc94bb2077edde5907976bdb9f280a931c5027.json:798:            "P-256 (secp256r1) is secure today but vulnerable to Shor's algorithm on a sufficiently large quantum computer.",
requiredActions: P-256 (secp256r1) is secure today but vulnerable to Shor's algorithm on a sufficiently large quantum computer.
NIST post-quantum signature standards: ML-DSA (FIPS 204) and SLH-DSA (FIPS 205) are the recommended replacements.
For new systems handling sensitive long-lived data, evaluate hybrid ECDSA+ML-DSA or pure ML-DSA.

finding.title: GCM nonce reuse risk detected — mutable or time-based IV/nonce near AES-GCM cipher
evidence: src/mcp/server.ts:1901:        const body = { runId, gateFailed, findingCount, criticalCount, timestamp: new Date().toISOString() };
requiredActions: Generate a fresh random 12-byte nonce for every encryption with crypto.randomBytes(12).
GCM nonce reuse completely breaks confidentiality and authentication.

finding.title: rejectUnauthorized: false disables TLS certificate verification, enabling MITM attacks.
evidence: .mcp/baselines/0ef307bb4a01d514ff6ab3e50e73bbf5d34a564d.json:1854:          "title": "rejectUnauthorized: false disables TLS certificate verification, enabling MITM attacks.",

.mcp/baselines/0ef307bb4a01d514ff6ab3e50e73bbf5d34a564d.json:1857:            ".mcp/baselines/0ef307bb4a01d514ff6ab3e50e73bbf5d34a564d.json:1838:          \"title\": \"rejectUnauthorized: false disables TLS certificate verification, enabling MITM attacks.\",",
.mcp/baselines/0ef307bb4a01d514ff6ab3e50e73bbf5d34a564d.json:1872:            "Remove rejectUnauthorized: false and fix the underlying certificate issue.",
.mcp/baselines/latest.json:1854:          "title": "rejectUnauthorized: false disables TLS certificate verification, enabling MITM attacks.",

.mcp/baselines/latest.json:1857:            ".mcp/baselines/0ef307bb4a01d514ff6ab3e50e73bbf5d34a564d.json:1838:          \"title\": \"rejectUnauthorized: false disables TLS certificate verification, enabling MITM attacks.\",",
.mcp/baselines/latest.json:1872:            "Remove rejectUnauthorized: false and fix the underlying certificate issue.",
requiredActions: Remove rejectUnauthorized: false and fix the underlying certificate issue.
If using a self-signed cert, add it via the ca option rather than disabling verification.

finding.title: Full request body logged — may expose PII/credentials
evidence: .github/security-exceptions-ci.json:72:      "justification": "These findings come from the REMEDIATION_MAP in src/mcp/server.ts, which contains intentional 'before' example code patterns (e.g., sslmode=disable, md5 hashing, console.log(req.body), postgresql://user:pass@host) us

.mcp/baselines/0ef307bb4a01d514ff6ab3e50e73bbf5d34a564d.json:1552:            ".github/security-exceptions-ci.json:72:      \"justification\": \"These findings come from the REMEDIATION_MAP in src/mcp/server.ts, which contains intentional 'before' example code patterns (e.g., sslmode=disable, md5 hashing,

.mcp/baselines/0ef307bb4a01d514ff6ab3e50e73bbf5d34a564d.json:1887:            ".github/security-exceptions-ci.json:72:      \"justification\": \"These findings come from the REMEDIATION_MAP in src/mcp/server.ts, which contains intentional 'before' example code patterns (e.g., sslmode=disable, md5 hashing,

.mcp/baselines/6abc94bb2077edde5907976bdb9f280a931c5027.json:604:            ".github/security-exceptions-ci.json:66:      \"justification\": \"These findings come from the REMEDIATION_MAP in src/mcp/server.ts, which contains intentional 'before' example code patterns (e.g., sslmode=disable, md5 hashing,

.mcp/baselines/6abc94bb2077edde5907976bdb9f280a931c5027.json:814:            ".github/security-exceptions-ci.json:66:      \"justification\": \"These findings come from the REMEDIATION_MAP in src/mcp/server.ts, which contains intentional 'before' example code patterns (e.g., sslmode=disable, md5 hashing,
requiredActions: Never log full request bodies — use field allowlists to log only non-sensitive fields.
GDPR Article 5: data minimization applies to logs. HIPAA prohibits logging PHI.

finding.title: Stack trace exposed in API response — CWE-209 information leakage
requiredActions: Never expose stack traces in API responses (CWE-209).
Log errors internally with a correlation ID; return only a safe error message to clients.

finding.title: No SBOM found. Syft is available — run it to generate one.
requiredActions: Run: syft . -o cyclonedx-json=.mcp/sbom/latest.json
Or set SECURITY_AUTO_SBOM=true to auto-generate on each gate run.

finding.title: No SLSA provenance attestation found
requiredActions: Generate SLSA provenance during CI/CD build.
Use slsa-github-generator or equivalent to produce .intoto.jsonl attestations.
Store provenance in .mcp/attestations/

finding.title: IR playbook missing: Web compromise (security/playbooks/web-compromise.md)
requiredActions: Create the IR playbook at security/playbooks/web-compromise.md.
Include all required sections: detection criteria, escalation path, containment, eradication, recovery, communication, post-incident review, and MTTD/MTTR targets.

finding.title: IR playbook missing: Cloud misconfiguration (security/playbooks/cloud-misconfiguration.md)
requiredActions: Create the IR playbook at security/playbooks/cloud-misconfiguration.md.
Include all required sections: detection criteria, escalation path, containment, eradication, recovery, communication, post-incident review, and MTTD/MTTR targets.

finding.title: IR playbook missing: Ransomware (security/playbooks/ransomware.md)
requiredActions: Create the IR playbook at security/playbooks/ransomware.md.
Include all required sections: detection criteria, escalation path, containment, eradication, recovery, communication, post-incident review, and MTTD/MTTR targets.

finding.title: GitHub Actions workflows without explicit permissions block
requiredActions: Add an explicit `permissions:` block at the top of each workflow (or at the job level) to grant only the minimum required scopes.
Without explicit permissions, the default is determined by org/repo settings — often write-all.
Example minimal read-only: `permissions: { contents: read }`.

finding.title: Log injection — user-controlled string written to logs without newline sanitization (CWE-117)
requiredActions: Strip or encode \n and \r from user-controlled values before logging.
CWE-117 — log injection forges log entries, erasing evidence of attacks or injecting false audit trails.
Fix: logger.info('Login attempt', { username: username.replace(/[\r\n]/g, '_') });

finding.title: SSRF — HTTP request to user-controlled URL without allowlist (CWE-918)
requiredActions: Validate the URL hostname against an explicit allowlist before making server-side HTTP requests.
CWE-918 / ATT&CK T1090 — SSRF reaches 169.254.169.254 for cloud metadata, internal services, and localhost.
Fix: const { hostname } = new URL(userUrl); if (!ALLOWED_HOSTS.includes(hostname)) throw new Error('Blocked');

finding.title: Command injection — child_process called with user-controlled input or shell:true (CWE-78)
evidence: src/cli/onboarding.ts:290:  const result = spawnSync(cmd, args, { stdio: "inherit" });
requiredActions: Use execFile() with a static path and an array of validated arguments — never string concatenation.
CWE-78 / ATT&CK T1059 — command injection achieves full OS compromise via shell metacharacters (;, |, $(), \n).
Fix: execFile('/usr/bin/convert', ['-resize', validatedSize, inputFile, outputFile], { shell: false });

finding.title: ReDoS — user-controlled input used to construct RegExp — catastrophic backtracking (CWE-1333)
evidence: src/repo/search.ts:52:	return new RegExp(pattern, "i"); // throws SyntaxError on invalid patterns
requiredActions: Never construct RegExp from user input. Escape with escape-string-regexp, or use string.includes() / startsWith().
CWE-1333 / ATT&CK T1499 — a crafted pattern like (a+)+ causes exponential backtracking that hangs the Node.js event loop.
Fix: const safe = escapeStringRegexp(userInput); const re = new RegExp(safe);

finding.title: SQL injection — SQL keyword with template literal interpolation (CWE-89)
evidence: src/mcp/learning.ts:305:      console.error(`[security-mcp] SECURITY_ALERT: Global suppression cap reached. ${suppressedCount} finding types already suppressed. Rejecting FP update for ${validated.findingId}. Possible learning-system attack.`);

src/mcp/learning.ts:328:    warning = `LEARNING_ANOMALY_HIGH_FP_RATE: Finding ${validated.findingId} has a false-positive rate of ${Math.round(updated.falsePositiveRate * 100)}% across ${updated.sampleSize} samples. Investigate scanner accuracy.`;
src/mcp/orchestration.ts:731:    if (parsed) console.warn(`[checkUpdates] Ignoring malformed version string from npm registry: ${JSON.stringify(parsed)}`);
src/mcp/orchestration.ts:806:      ...skillUpdates.map((s) => `# skill: ${s.skillName} will be refreshed via orchestration.ensure_skill`)
src/mcp/orchestration.ts:812:      ? `Run the following commands to update:\n${commands.filter((c) => !c.startsWith("#")).join("\n")}`
requiredActions: Never interpolate user input into SQL strings. Use parameterized queries or prepared statements exclusively.
CWE-89 / ATT&CK T1190 — SQL injection enables authentication bypass, data exfiltration, and database destruction.
Fix: db.query('SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = $1', [userId]) or use an ORM with parameterized inputs.

finding.title: SQL injection — SQL query built via string concatenation with user input (CWE-89)
evidence: src/mcp/server.ts:2027:    pattern: "db.query('SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ' + req.params.id)",
requiredActions: Replace string concatenation in SQL queries with parameterized queries or prepared statements.
CWE-89 / ATT&CK T1190 — ' OR '1'='1 via string concatenation bypasses authentication entirely.
Fix: use db.query('SELECT * FROM users WHERE name = ?', [name]) — never string concatenation.

finding.title: jwt.verify() called without explicit algorithms array — algorithm confusion attack possible (CWE-327)
evidence: src/mcp/server.ts:915:- [ ] JWT algorithm pinned to RS256 or ES256 in all jwt.verify() calls (CWE-327)
requiredActions: Always pass algorithms: ['RS256'] (or your actual algorithm) to jwt.verify().
CWE-327 — without algorithms pin, attacker can forge tokens using alg:none or switch RS256→HS256 using the public key as secret.
Fix: jwt.verify(token, publicKey, { algorithms: ['RS256'] })

finding.title: jwt.sign() called without expiresIn — tokens never expire (CWE-613)
requiredActions: Always set an expiry on JWTs: jwt.sign(payload, secret, { expiresIn: '1h' }).
CWE-613 — a JWT without expiresIn remains valid indefinitely, even after account compromise.
Fix: jwt.sign(payload, process.env.JWT_SECRET!, { algorithms: ['RS256'], expiresIn: '1h' })

finding.title: jwt.verify() without algorithms array locked to an asymmetric algorithm — algorithm confusion vector (CWE-327)
evidence: src/mcp/server.ts:915:- [ ] JWT algorithm pinned to RS256 or ES256 in all jwt.verify() calls (CWE-327)
requiredActions: Explicitly set algorithms: ['RS256'] or ['ES256'] (or your asymmetric algorithm) in jwt.verify() options.
CWE-327 — without an algorithm pin, the library will accept whatever algorithm the token header specifies.
Fix: jwt.verify(token, publicKey, { algorithms: ['RS256'] })

finding.title: require() called with a non-literal specifier — dynamic module loading (CWE-95 / CWE-706)
evidence: src/cli/index.ts:27:    const pkg = require(resolve(__dirname, "../../package.json")) as { version: string };
requiredActions: Replace dynamic require() with a static allowlist: const mods = { a: require('./a'), b: require('./b') }; use mods[key].
CWE-95 / ATT&CK T1059.007 — an attacker controlling the specifier can load ../../.env or any file on disk.
Enable --experimental-require-module tree shaking at build time to make dynamic require detectable at the bundler level.

finding.title: MD5 or SHA-1 used in crypto.createHash/createCipher — broken hash algorithm (CWE-327)
requiredActions: MD5 and SHA-1 are cryptographically broken — collision attacks are practical and documented (Shattered, SLOTH).
CWE-327 / ATT&CK T1600 — weak hashes used for password storage, HMAC, or digital signature allow forgery.
Replace with crypto.createHash('sha256') for integrity, scrypt/argon2 for passwords, and AES-256-GCM for symmetric encryption.

finding.title: Hardcoded IP address in production code — infrastructure coupling and reconnaissance aid (CWE-1188)
requiredActions: Hardcoded IPs expose internal topology, break across environments, and become stale without code changes.
CWE-1188 / ATT&CK T1592.002 — exposed IPs aid attacker reconnaissance and pivot targeting.
Replace with environment variables (process.env.SERVICE_HOST) or DNS names resolved at runtime. Use 0.0.0.0 for bind addresses.

controlCoverage.description: Incident response playbooks exist for all surfaces, are tested within 90 days, and have contact lists.
Container images comply with CIS Docker Benchmark Level 2.
Kubernetes cluster configuration complies with CIS Kubernetes Benchmark Level 2.
Cloud account configuration complies with CIS Benchmark Level 2 for the active cloud provider.
Kubernetes pods enforce security contexts with least-privilege settings.
Kubernetes NetworkPolicy resources restrict pod-to-pod traffic.
All database connections require TLS/SSL encryption in transit.
Database credentials use least-privilege accounts, not root/admin.
Only NIST-approved cryptographic algorithms are used (AES-256, SHA-256+, RSA-2048+).
PII, PAN, SSN, and credentials are never written to application logs.

confidence.summary: Automated coverage 66%, scanner readiness 86%, missing controls 5, risk-accepted controls 25. Change type: auth.

resolvedFindings.title: Compiled dist/ directory excluded from secret scan
evidence: dist/ directory exists but is excluded from secret scanning
requiredActions: Manually inspect dist/ for secrets injected by build tools such as webpack DefinePlugin or Vite define.
Ensure secrets are not inlined into compiled bundles via build-time substitution.
Consider adding a targeted scan of dist/ for high-confidence patterns (API key prefixes, PEM headers) in CI.

requiredActions: Enable audit logging on all cloud resources and ship logs to a centralised, tamper-evident store.
Enable deletion protection on databases and stateful resources.
Retain audit logs for at least 1 year (SOC 2 / PCI DSS requirement).

resolvedFindings.title: No GraphQL fragment depth limiting detected — circular fragment DoS risk
requiredActions: Add fragment depth limiting via a custom validation rule.
Use graphql-depth-limit or implement NoSchemaIntrospectionCustomRule with fragment cycle detection.
Circular fragments can be used to exhaust server resources.
